what_it_does: Defines CSS for an imagery subsystem covering header pills, tab navigation, toolbars, image grids/cards, project and hub rails, panorama thumbnails and hotspots, a 360 viewer, AI Studio panels, prompt/source/composition controls, result/history/chat views, mask editing, and an image editor modal.
observations: Uses a warm paper, ink, bronze, and gold token palette throughout the shown rules.
Organizes the file into large commented sections for imagery navigation, grids, projects, hubs, panorama viewer, AI Studio, mask editor, and image editor.
Defines many component-scoped class families, especially cv-im, cv-as, cv-pano, cv-mask, and cv-ed.
Uses CSS grid and flex layouts extensively for rails, cards, toolbars, tabs, result grids, modals, and editor shells.
Includes interactive state styling for hover, active, disabled, focus, loading, running, success, warning, and error states.
Contains keyframe animations for panorama pins, AI pipeline running dots, and editor backdrop fade-in.

Styles complex product surfaces such as resizable AI Studio columns, image source pickers, composition tiles, generation parameter controls, sticky run bars, chat bubbles, mask canvas controls, crop overlays, and filter grids.
standouts: The shown CSS covers several full workflows rather than only a small component set.
The AI Studio section is especially broad, with preset rails, pipelines, prompt input, image selection, composition, generation parameters, results, history, chat, and modal styles.
The image editor and mask editor rules include task-specific interaction affordances such as crop handles, canvas staging, brush size controls, and editor mode tabs.
Panorama styling includes thumbnails, hotspot lists, loading overlays, pulse pins, and coordinate rows.
conventions: Uses BEM-like modifier classes such as .ok, .warn, .active, .running, .primary, .icon, .low, .fail, and --sm.
Relies heavily on design tokens for spacing, radius, typography, shadows, status colors, paper/ink colors, and gold accents.
Groups related rules under banner comments with uppercase subsystem headings.
Uses short descendant selectors inside component namespaces for labels, metadata, actions, rows, buttons, and images.
Mixes paper/ink token names with bg/fg token names in the same file.
Uses compact one-line declarations for many simple selectors and expanded blocks for larger component rules.
concerns: The file is large and spans multiple distinct surfaces, which makes local scanning and isolated maintenance harder.
Several controls are styled primarily through hover and active states, with focus styling present only on some inputs and textareas.
Some color values are hardcoded rgba literals instead of tokenized variables.
The selector .cv-resizable-col:nth-child(5) couples result grid behavior to a specific DOM position.
The shown file repeats similar tab, button, panel, card, and modal patterns across namespaces without a local shared abstraction.
notes: This file is the visual CSS layer for ConceptV imagery, panorama, AI image generation, mask editing, and image editing surfaces.
questions: What markup structure owns the cv-resizable-col:nth-child(5) assumption?
Are the bg/fg tokens and paper/ink tokens intentionally mixed as two palette layers?
Does the truncated remainder continue the image editor filter/export/share rules?
Are keyboard focus states handled elsewhere for icon buttons, tab buttons, tiles, and overlay actions?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the shown content: the file extends a warm paper and gold ink visual system across imagery, settings-like controls, editor surfaces, AI Studio, and a 360 viewer.
intention_signals: The file appears intended to make imagery work feel like an integrated studio workspace, with asset browsing, project/hub organization, AI generation, mask editing, panorama inspection, and image editing sharing the same restrained visual language.
novelty: The distinct aspect is the combination of asset library CSS, panorama hotspot UI, AI generation workflow UI, chat/results/history surfaces, mask canvas controls, and crop-based image editor styling in one file-local stylesheet.
contribution: Contributes a cohesive set of CSS substrates for visual asset workflows: warm-paper imagery grids, project and hub navigation, panorama viewer affordances, AI Studio generation controls, result/history/chat layouts, mask editor controls, and modal image editor mechanics.
summary_for_embedding: CSS stylesheet for ConceptV imagery workflows, including asset grids, project and hub rails, panorama viewer hotspots, AI Studio generation UI, mask editor controls, and image editor modal styling.
intention_for_embedding: It was for giving image management, AI image creation, 360 viewing, masking, and editing tools a shared warm studio design-system treatment.
